vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone

requirements: Structural integrity under wing flex
Cooling efficiency
Service access ease

risk.desc: Potential structural weakness at the hinge point of the equipment bay door.
risk.mit: Increase the thickness of the door hinge area.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient cooling due to inadequate cooling inlet design.
risk.mit: Optimize the cooling inlet design for better airflow.

risk.desc: Possibility of misalignment during assembly due to loose tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement tighter manufacturing tolerances.

risk.desc: Risk of hidden interfaces within the monolithic body affecting service access.
risk.mit: Ensure all removable modules and inspection paths are clearly visible.

risk.desc: Potential for thermal stress on the motor mount due to direct exposure.
risk.mit: Add thermal insulation around the motor mount area.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

physics_paths.path: Motor heat transfers through the pod structure to the cooling inlet.
Wing flex causes relative motion between the pod and the wing structure.
Access to internal electronics via the equipment bay door.

integration_opportunities.description: Integrate sensors into the sensor_pocket for monitoring internal conditions.
Optimize the cooling_inlet design for better airflow and heat dissipation.
Improve accessibility to internal components via the access_window.

manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for alignment and fit

generated_parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
generated_parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

requirements: Structures: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path
Interfaces: spar_socket, anti_rotation_key, rib, carry_through, frame, sensor_pocket, service_access, mounting_datum, harness_interface
Datum visibility: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled
Clearance named: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances
Interface count: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums
Standard fit vocabulary: Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance
Standard hardware dimensioning: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes
Spar datum access: Spar socket, anti-rotation keys, and center datum remain visible and inspectable
Mission payload non-weapon boundary: Payload bay is labeled non-weapon/display-only with removable mission/sensor module
CG battery service: Battery bay uses slide rail, CG marks, and independent payload/battery removal
Load path continuity: Lift, landing, and payload loads trace through continuous structure: spar to carry-through to fuselage frames, with fittings at every load transfer

verify.rule: All required evidence features present
Expected mass within budget
All parts and features traceable to original design intent
All interfaces named and reciprocal
Visible locking or preload evidence on critical joints

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to fixed interfaces
risk.mit: Implement thermal expansion gaps and flexible joints

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to complex interfaces
risk.mit: Develop detailed assembly instructions and training

risk.desc: Possibility of structural failure under extreme loading conditions
risk.mit: Conduct additional stress analysis and testing

risk.desc: Risk of improper fit due to manufacturing tolerances
risk.mit: Implement tighter manufacturing controls and inspections

risk.desc: Potential for interference between moving parts
risk.mit: Review clearance fits and adjust as necessary

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path

physics_paths.description: Lift, landing, and payload loads trace through continuous structure
Thermal expansion and contraction managed by anti-rotation key

generated_parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
generated_parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

requirements: Provide structural support for the wing's airfoil profile
Include lightening pockets for weight reduction
Feature a leading edge sock for aerodynamic efficiency
Implement a trailing edge taper for improved aerodynamics
Incorporate control surface cutouts with hinges
Ensure service access for battery replacement
Maintain thermal dissipation from EPP foam core to ambient air
Support payload bay with non-weapon/display-only labeling

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to mixed materials (EPP foam core and CFRP spar).
risk.mit: Conduct thermal expansion analysis and adjust design if necessary.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient structural integrity if lightening pockets are too large.
risk.mit: Perform finite element analysis to validate structural strength.

risk.desc: Battery access cutout may interfere with structural integrity.
risk.mit: Evaluate alternative locations for battery access cutout.

risk.desc: Uncertainty in precise alignment of spar socket during assembly.
risk.mit: Implement additional alignment aids or improve press-fit tolerance.

risk.desc: Risk of improper service accessibility due to complex geometry.
risk.mit: Conduct physical mockup testing to ensure ease of access.

physics_paths.path: Lift from airfoil section to spar, then to wing root
Heat dissipation from EPP foam core to ambient air
Battery access via wing rib cutout
